speaker: MATT
raw_text: You can see people that are fleeing from the town in little bits, two or three at a time, trails of individuals that are just now managing to make it outside of the city of Emon itself. Some of them are heading out into the distance, getting far away as possible. A few more are heading towards the keep, and you gather that this crowd has been gathering here for the last few minutes, at the very least.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How many people am I seeing down there?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, as you get up to the top and crest over, you can see the three guards are all yelling at each other and yelling over, "We can't, we can't do anything right now, we're waiting for th--" They look over and see you come over the edge, and you can see there's probably close to 50 or so people that are all pressed up against the gates to the inside of your keep, that are all shouting to be let in.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: If I look past, do I see anyone else coming towards the keep? Is it just a cluster and that's it, or--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So 42 points of damage. Still not shabby by any means. Gotcha, as you chuck both daggers in the direction where you can see as it's now at the very bottom of the fog cloud. You have enough visual to strike towards it without missing and both daggers sink into the bottom of the creature. One of them you know was a nice, impactful wounding for any other creature, but it doesn't seem to have taken much issue with the impact of the blade. Appear at your side again. That ends your reaction. Now Vorugal is going to go. Looking at the area of most density--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A surprise generally means that it has absolutely no idea that you are there or there's any sort of threat from you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24 hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. And then the second dagger I throw-- I can only throw a second one-- is also a 24.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Both hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So the first one is double dice, so eight plus-- 14, 15, 18, 23, 25. Double dice is 50 plus seven is-- Yeah, 57 for the first.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And the second one goes bink. That is a ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ten, all right. 57 points of damage, that's-- Is that straight-up dice rolls? That's crazy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's Sneak Attack, I've gone up a level to 13--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right, so it's a d6 or 7d6.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 7d6-- Oh, wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's not doubled, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, sorry, sorry. 25 plus seven is 32. Sorry, 32.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 32, there you go, I was like, that's really high for a non-crit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The second one was ten. 32, ten.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. So as you focus and look up, you can see now some of the fog cloud that surrounds it congeals into a darker color and clings to the dragon's form as it is now Hexed by you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'm going to drop down-- Like a boss.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Are you going to leap or climb down?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to leap.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, and roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 29, success and managed to avoid one of your guards who was right below you. You weren't aware and managed to dodge off to the side this way and use the edge of the wall to kick off to change the trajectory, as you land, carrying Bad News in one hand, three-point landing in the other.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sick, Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hold that gun up from the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep. Just making sure it doesn't damage itself.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, the next thing I'm going to do is, I'm going to concentrate really hard and I'm going to try and conjure Hex to see if it works.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's been too long, too long have I been here, I do not know who I am."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, Freddie, I imagine I'm wondering the same thing you are, at least I hope, and that's: what's in it for the skull?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He wants to be freed. It. How would we free you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's a slight pause before you can see the gem, the swirl coalesces into what looks like a spectral face. You can make out what looks to be eyes, a mouth and a nose. Though the nose itself is less of a pronounced human nose and--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm looking at the floor, I'm not looking at it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. But you see where the nose is, instead there are two nasal passages. And you hear a voice slowly creak out from it and say, "All that I require is for you to destroy this prison. Free my spirit from generations of torment, that is all I ask."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey, quick question, who are you? Who were you before you were in this prison?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. So you go and plant a protective position in front of her? It's now your turn. Grog, who rushes over and shoulder slams Percy out of the way, and he's fallen to the ground. His gun almost scatters to the floor of the workshop around you. As you're expecting, he turns towards you, instead he steps and his giant frame is now blocking you with the shoulder blades in front, his arms out in a defensive position, facing off with people as they begin to enter the room. What are you going to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Why can't that be higher? 19.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Ah shit, 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 14, I need you to go ahead and make a strength saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit. 17, oh, saving throw. 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 21. You slam into him and you essentially shove him with the shoulder. You manage to knock him prone, but the skull doesn't come loose, he loses his grip on it, and currently Vex is still holding it front of you. You're prone on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm prone on the ground?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So Vex still has it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: But it's Vex's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: But I knocked him off of her. But you've still got the skull.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I wasn't trying to wrestle it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Anything else you want to try and accomplish?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I would like to turn and put my back to Vex, because she has the skull.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point she nods and goes, "I know not the middle two, but Vorugal has been known to wander the wastes north in Othanzia. Some of the offspring have wandered further, in fact I believe the Skysunder that you destroyed naught but two years ago that was sealed in the Frostweald was one of its brethren. Its children. But Vorugal has not been seen wandering this far out. Chromatics do not work together, they are selfish and greedy and territorial."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do we do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And at this point, she looks around for a chair. You can see her hands are shaking, and she goes and grabs a chair and pulls it up and sits down for a second, puts her hands on her knees, and quietly, "Describe to me these dragons."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There was one of each color.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: A green, a black, a white.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And a big red piece of shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All colors except for blue of the chromatic spectrum. I got a few of their names.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you go through each color, she nods, but when you say the big red, her body freezes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does this sound familiar to you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The names, you said?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There's a white dragon that called himself Vorugal. A green dragon, Raishan. Black dragon, Umbrasyl, and the red dragon, we haven't heard his name yet. He seems to be the leader.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Now for the first time, you have known Allura for quite some time, she's been a very stoic, strong, confident, and powerful person. At this moment, she has withered. She looks genuinely like she's hit a level of fear you have never seen in an individual like this, and it shakes you to see this upon her face, and she puts her hand up and says, "This is not your fault. This burden is mine." And looks over to the dwarf and says, "It is ours." And the dwarf, you can now see, he's nodding in understanding. "This dragon... it's been called many names, but the one that it's taken is Thordak, The Cinder King."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They're definitely working together.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "This red you say is the leader?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, most definitely.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He had a message that he belted over the entire city. He said that we who live, he's showing mercy and that we can either fight or flee, and then--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He said if we fight or flee, we'll be denied the mercy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, something along those lines. And then he said that it was the guardians of this city that brought this upon Emon, and that he has control. He called it like a new order.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He said our new purpose is to serve him.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Thordak. This terrible beast lorded over half of Marquet for centuries, taking the scalding sands as its domain and its denizens as cattle, and just as the city of Ank'harel was becoming the jewel of the desert, Thordak struck. A battle, however, that it was unable to win. Sent away, mortally wounded, it was thought that the dragon had died soon after, having not been seen for many centuries since. That is, until about 15 years ago. The rumors of a powerful drake, storming the beaches and seas, had been whispered for a long time, while ships would be lost at sea. It had survived and secretly ruled the island of Viscan in the south of the Lucidian Ocean. I suppose after generations, the dragon, now self-crowned as the Cinder King, grew greedier with the island's limited resources and began razing the Mournset countryside south of the Verdant Expanse and the Stormcrest Mountains." And her eyes get even more distant as she is recalling all this. "Reports of a dragon more massive than the records can compare hit Tal'Dorei. At the time, I wasn't part of the council. I traveled with a group of adventurers much like yourselves: Kima, Drake here, and a handful of other brave souls who are no longer with us. We traveled to investigate, but arrived to find this creature destroying everything surrounding the Glade Pools. Port Udall, gone, Byroden a smoldering ruin, and we gathered a small army of mercenaries and hunted it back to its new den in the Stormcrest, where a large number of lizardfolk had formed around him. But we were overpowered by the sheer might of the dragon. Many lives were lost. Knowing that we could not kill it, we turned to other means. I called upon the Arcana Pansophical, a circle of friends, arcane practitioners that agreed to work together across the land to keep them safe. They devised a trap, one that could, we believed, permanently seal Thordak within the elemental plane of fire, preventing him from ever returning to our world. At the sacrifice of many, including our companions Sirus, Dohla, Ghenn, we succeeded." And as the tears begin to roll down her face, she looks around. "For naught now, it seems, and somehow he has broken our invocation. We bound his essence to the fountains of creation and thought he would stay there forever." And she goes quiet and looks around for a second. "I'm sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He said our new purpose is to serve him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Now for the first time, you have known Allura for quite some time, she's been a very stoic, strong, confident, and powerful person. At this moment, she has withered. She looks genuinely like she's hit a level of fear you have never seen in an individual like this, and it shakes you to see this upon her face, and she puts her hand up and says, "This is not your fault. This burden is mine." And looks over to the dwarf and says, "It is ours." And the dwarf, you can now see, he's nodding in understanding. "This dragon... it's been called many names, but the one that it's taken is Thordak, The Cinder King."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thordak?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Allura looks at you dead-on and says, "The only guardians that Thordak would know of would be the ones that sent him away to that plane 15 years ago. This is my fault."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Chromatics are selfish beings, they do not care for each other, and the power of one is enough, but a conclave of dragons is both extraordinary and horrifying, but there is no-- There is no sense of vengeance for their kin unless it is against the greater plan. Now, if what you say is true and these other dragons are bound together in the service of Thordak, I imagine they would be the ones who had the power and the resources to find some way to bring him through."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At which point, the dwarf steps up and still has his hand rubbing Allura's shoulder and says, "We knew not what we were doing, other than trying to save it, don't blame yourself."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Allura.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Would you consider us guardians of this city?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "One of many. I believe the guardians--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is this because of us?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's been over a year at least. Well, if it's taken them that long at least to free Thordak, it means that this was probably in the works for quite some time. If we do share this responsibility, I assure you it weighs heavier on me than it does you. But right now we need all the allies we can gather. Now, you say Thordak is where right now?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is this because of us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Allura looks at you dead-on and says, "The only guardians that Thordak would know of would be the ones that sent him away to that plane 15 years ago. This is my fault."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's not true. If we are to solve this situation we cannot hide the truth. I killed Krieg. We killed Krieg. I finished Krieg.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Krieg was a dragon, Allura.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: A blue dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We stumbled upon his lair, reached through his home in Emon, through magical means. We found his lair.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He attacked us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And we-- I--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I reach out and I grab Vax's hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We survived.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: When it happened, he had four stones, four orbs in his lair. They saw us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They spoke out to us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: They threatened us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "When was this? Was this--"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Many years ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe two years ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not all that long ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It was right after we got to Emon.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, you guys can begin to take a short rest, if you want to heal up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, yes, the best I can, yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I want to scry and first check in on the Fire Ashari.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. All right, at this point she pulls out her crystal ball from a little tiny satchel, which is in itself a smaller Bag of Holding. Pulls out a small mirror, takes some water and pours it on the mirror, places the sphere above it, and it floats for a second. And there's a makeshift scrying ritual site here before you. You guys take about ten minutes to concentrate, and if you guys want to do anything in that ten-minute period, you're welcome to, or if you just want to watch and wait, you can.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to just sit, it's been a long, long day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. The spell completes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I roll hit dice?
